rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just an approve), so both candidates correctly land on APPROVE with equal recall. But X cites specific test-file line numbers (2638, 2651, 2720) that don't match the actual diff hunks (which fall around 2533/2546/2615) and invokes an unverifiable, suspiciously specific upstream PR number (vllm#46022) plus upstream file/line refs — signs of fabricated grounding. Y's line-by-line citations (754, 1017, 1157, 1248, 1286, 1329, and 2533/2546/2615) match the diff almost exactly, though it mislabels the api_server.py count as '4' instead of 6, a minor slip rather than a location error. Y's higher grounding accuracy makes it more trustworthy despite similar verbosity and identical final verdicts.
